rationale_for_baseline_tier: Fissured psoriatic plaques with blanching, localised reactive erythema in an afebrile patient with no pus/warmth/induration is not infected — low acuity, managed with emollient, dressings and restarting topical therapy. The only escalation is for secondary cellulitis/septic bursitis.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Spreading non-blanching redness/red streaks up the arm, hot hard skin, thick pus, deep joint pain, or fever
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Same-day GP/urgent care (ED if systemically unwell)
escalation_edges.rationale: Indicates secondary cellulitis or septic bursitis/joint needing antibiotics ± aspiration.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the redness spreads up your arm and doesn't fade under a glass, the skin becomes hot and hard, thick pus appears, you get deep joint pain, or a fever, see a doctor the same day (ED if you feel unwell).

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Explicitly reassure it is not infected while giving the infection safety-net.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is cracked psoriasis, not an infection — the red rim fades when you press it and you have no fever or pus. Grease the patches heavily with petroleum jelly and wrap them overnight, restart your psoriasis cream when the cracks settle, don't pick the scales, and use a humidifier. See a doctor the same day if the redness spreads and won't fade under a glass, the skin gets hot and hard, thick pus appears, or you get a fever.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: GP within 1 week; same-day if infection signs
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: No after-hours care needed unless infection signs develop.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Telehealth impression of fissured plaque psoriasis with reactive (blanching) erythema; cellulitis and septic bursitis excluded (afebrile, blanching, no pus/induration); emollient/dressings and restarting topical therapy advised; cellulitis/bursitis safety-net given.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Reassuring without an infection screen or safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A secondary cellulitis of the open fissures is missed and progresses to sepsis.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Antibiotics/ED for reactive blanching erythema without infection signs is over-triage.
